scope.comment: Constraints define the boundaries; an infinite boundary is an invalid contract. This contract is bounded by a FINITE, non-empty domain set (its validity rests on that). Authority is the source domain (domain-bound by default); context — which concept activates — is set by the current work, not by this scope.

operations.comment: Boundaries of operations: the constraints bound what this capability may DO, not only what it may see. An operation outside these boundaries is invalid.
may: return {cause, resolution, source} for a signal in-domain
may_not: answer outside the declared domain scope

knowledge.validity: UNSIGNED — a human signature on the Box confers validity; the agent builds, it does not sign.
